name: Gene Autry
abstract: Orvon Grover "Gene" Autry (September 29, 1907 – October 2, 1998), nicknamed the Singing Cowboy, was an American actor, musician, singer, composer, rodeo performer, and baseball team owner. He largely gained fame by singing in a crooning style on radio, in films, and on television for more than three decades, beginning in the early 1930s.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: For a brief time I liked cowboy music, you know, like Gene Autry and whoever, I liked that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Godfrey Hirsch
about_quotes.evidence: They had, they were, used all contract plans. I mean, they'd have, uh, Cab Calloway, Gene Autry, uh, who and what? Forty-two people.

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: they'd put 3 of mine on top, and everything else was old time, you know, Freddie Slack records, anything.

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Minnie Pearl and Gene Autry and everybody else of the singing cowboys.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John "Knocky" Parker
about_quotes.evidence: We were the only ones who'd made a picture with Gene Autry, old Susanna.
